pr.title: Improve matching in Melee source files
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Large batch decomp/matching PR touching 57 src/melee files across effect, fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, menu, map, trophy, library, and player systems. The PR body reports saved-baseline progress, require-protos validation, ninja changes_all, 13 local new matches, 123 unmatched-item improvements, and 0 broken/unmatched regressions for the author's local run. Review feedback focused on avoiding pragmas, replacing repeated generated-looking blocks with loops, removing M2C_FIELD where possible, treating jobj.h asserts as evidence for existing HSD_JObj helpers, and reverting a likely fake-match/regression in lb_00F9 plus tooling changes.

key_files.role: largest changed file; PR body reports main/melee/mp/mplib .data improved from 60.80% to 94.04% and mpGetSpeed reached 100.00%
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:new matches and unmatched improvements tables

key_files.role: large result-screen matching work around fn_80175DC8/fn_80174B4C; review flagged possible loop and M2C_FIELD cleanup
match-end/game-mode matching work; review flagged pragma avoidance and repeated code likely being a loop
stage matching work with large function/section progress; review summary notes probable loop feedback
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:grKongo_801D7134, grKongo_801D6AFC, grKongo_801D577C improvements and .rodata/.sdata new matches

key_files.role: stage matching work; review feedback connected jobj.h asserts to existing jobj functions
library matching work; review flagged an HSD_JObj-related change as likely regression/fake match
evidence_refs: metadata.review_feedback_examples:undo this likely regression/fake match; HSD_Jobj is the way it was written

key_files.role: effect matching tweaks; diff shows repeated local f32 temporary insertion before HSD_JObjSetRotationY and EF_ParamEntry cast for anim queue
effect matching tweaks mirroring efasync local f32 steering around HSD_JObjSetRotationY
fighter common effect-spawn matching cleanup; diff shows Vec3 locals replacing scalar component locals and removal of extra varargs/M2C_BITWISE-shaped artifacts
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:ftParts_GetBoneIndex direct assignment and efAsync_Spawn argument reductions

key_files.role: fighter common matching work; diff shows PAD_STACK/declaration/lifetime steering, explicit item loop, stage switch, and pragmas around ftCo_800A2718
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:ftCo_800A2718 pragma dont_inline, item loop, stage switch

key_files.role: tooling file mentioned only in review feedback as needing revert; not present in loaded changed_files list, so exact changed status is uncertain from this slice

what_changed: Changed 57 src/melee files, with 7984 additions and 7198 deletions reported by the PR slice metadata.
PR body states the work improved matching across a batch of src/melee source files and fixed regressions reported by saved-baseline comparison.
PR body states a strict local regression gate was added so changes/changes_all fail when any unit, section, or function metric moves backward.
Local report in PR body claims matched code moved to 70.39% (+0.24%, +9176 bytes) and matched data to 41.33% (+0.06%, +712 bytes).
Local report lists 13 new matches, 0 broken matches, 123 improvements in unmatched items, and 0 regressions in unmatched items.
Diff excerpt shows effect files using extra f32 temporaries before HSD_JObjSetRotationY calls, likely as narrow register/codegen steering.
Diff excerpt shows ftCo_09F7 replacing scalar stack component temporaries with Vec3 copies and simplifying efAsync_Spawn varargs at several call sites.
Diff excerpt shows gmresult adding a large static u8 table and expanding fn_80175DC8 with many local HSD_JObj temporaries, loops, switches, and PAD_STACK.
Review feedback indicates some generated-looking repeated blocks were expected to be tested as loops before landing.

smart_moves.text: The PR body included both the baseline commit and the pushed worktree commit plus the exact validation commands, making the matching claims auditable within the slice.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Clean PR worktree at ff551b3; Baseline from dae3629; python3 configure.py --require-protos; ninja changes_all

smart_moves.text: The author reported no broken matches and no unmatched-item regressions alongside the positive match deltas, which is the right ledger shape for broad matching batches.
Some fighter cleanup moved from scalar component locals to real Vec3 locals, improving type expression while preserving matching progress.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated player or jobj setup blocks, review should challenge pointer-stepped or copied generated-looking source by testing natural for-loop forms first.
Avoid leaving broad pragma-based matching controls in normal Melee source unless a narrow verifier-backed exception is unavoidable.
M2C_FIELD and generated raw field access should be treated as cleanup targets when a real struct field or local temporary type can express the access.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:ftCo_09F7 Vec3 locals replacing scalar component locals

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded jobj.h assert patterns can indicate the original source used an existing HSD_JObj helper; do not replace canonical HSD_JObj calls with fake or manually expanded matches without evidence.
evidence_refs: metadata.review_feedback_examples:src/melee/gr/grvenom.c any jobj.h asserts are indications of an included/run jobj.c function
metadata.review_feedback_examples:src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c undo this likely regression/fake match, the HSD_Jobj is the way it was written

decomp_lessons.lesson: Large matching batches need a saved-baseline regression ledger, not only local code-score improvements, because small data/section/function regressions can hide inside broad source churn.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Add a strict local regression gate so changes/changes_all fail when any unit, section, or function metric moves backward
human_pr_text:0 broken matches and 0 regressions in unmatched items

naming_conventions.convention: This PR largely kept address-style names for unresolved functions and labels, such as fn_80175DC8, fn_80161154, and lbl_803B7B18, rather than inventing unsupported semantics.
Where local roles were clear in diff excerpts, short role names appeared, such as jobj, gobj, fp, pdata, player, flags, model, and island_pos.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use narrow local temporaries to influence float argument/register shape, as shown by storing rot_y/half_pi into f32_1 or va_f32_1 before HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This is a matching tactic, not general style; it needs local objdiff/build evidence before being treated as acceptable source.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK and declaration-order changes were used in several diff excerpts to shape stack/layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: PAD_STACK and local lifetime/declaration churn require explicit verifier evidence and should be cleaned up when the underlying source shape is recovered.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Pragmas appeared as matching controls around selected functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Reviewer explicitly asked to avoid pragmas if possible; global optimizer/dont_inline pragmas should remain tightly scoped or be removed if not required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace scalar component locals with aggregate Vec3 locals when the source operation is naturally a vector copy plus component mutation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Supported in ftCo_09F7 excerpt, but should not be generalized without matching evidence in other files.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Large switch/lb_80011E24 jobj setup blocks may be candidates for table-driven or loop reconstruction, but review evidence only says to check loop forms; it does not prove the final intended source form.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Do not promote the specific gmresult structure as original style without full objdiff and local source comparison.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Repeated setup in fn_80175DC8 is probably a for loop; out-of-order 6/7 case makes it uncertain but worth checking.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Try to remove any M2C_FIELD if possible.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: jobj.h asserts are indications of included/run jobj.c functions; prefer mapping back to existing jobj operations.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Undo the highlighted change because it is likely a regression/fake match; HSD_JObj is the way it was written.

notes: Changed-file metadata and PR body are strong for file list, validation commands, local matching deltas, and broad classification.
Diff excerpt is truncated relative to a 751445-byte full diff, so file-specific implementation details are representative, not exhaustive.
Review comments file is truncated; only loaded comments and metadata review examples were used.
PR body reports local validation and expected CI confirmation; this intake does not independently verify builds, objdiff, or CI status.
tools/changes_fmt.py appears in review feedback examples but not in the loaded changed_files list; treat its changed status as uncertain in this slice.
Review feedback identifies likely loops, but the slice does not prove whether those comments were resolved before merge.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Review repeated decomp blocks for natural loops before accepting pointer-stepped copies
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2581, reviewer feedback on gm_1601, gmresult, and grkongo repeatedly challenged copied or pointer-stepped blocks as likely for-loops. Candidate lesson: repeated setup or slot-processing blocks in matching PRs should be tested as natural loops before being accepted as final source.

accepted_candidate_records.title: jobj.h assert expansions should be mapped back to canonical HSD_JObj helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2581 review feedback connected jobj.h asserts to existing jobj.c/HSD_JObj operations and flagged an lb_00F9 HSD_JObj change as likely fake-match regression. Candidate lesson: do not keep expanded jobj.h assert/helper bodies or fake replacements when a canonical HSD_JObj helper expresses the operation.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Broad matching batches need saved-baseline no-regression ledgers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2581 included baseline/worktree identifiers, require-protos, ninja changes_all, match deltas, new matches, unmatched improvements, and zero broken/unmatched regressions. Candidate lesson: batch matching PRs should present narrow validation plus regression accounting for unit, section, and function metric movement.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Add a strict local regression gate

source_update_candidates.title: Consider adding PR #2581 as supporting historical evidence for pragma avoidance
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer explicitly requested avoiding pragmas in gm_1601 where #pragma push and #pragma global_optimizer off appeared. This supports the existing avoid-pragmas-register-asm standard but does not by itself establish a new rule.
source_update_candidates.reason: Historical review evidence could be linked as an example under the existing standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider adding PR #2581 examples to natural-loop review guidance
source_update_candidates.text: Multiple review comments in this PR asked the author to check likely loops in repeated blocks, including gm_1601, gmresult, and grkongo.
source_update_candidates.reason: Good historical examples for the existing natural-loops standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider adding PR #2581 jobj.h assert review examples
source_update_candidates.text: Review feedback stated that jobj.h asserts indicate included/run jobj.c functions and flagged an HSD_JObj replacement in lb_00F9 as likely fake-match regression.
source_update_candidates.reason: Supports current header-inlines guidance with concrete review examples.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting strict saved-baseline regression gate wording if not already source-owned elsewhere
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2581 says it added a strict local regression gate so changes/changes_all fail when any unit, section, or function metric moves backward.
source_update_candidates.reason: May belong in tooling or contribution standards if this behavior is accepted project policy; the PR slice alone should not promote it as a global fact.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all 57 changed files were fully matched; the PR body only lists specific new matches and partial unmatched improvements.
Do not treat the use of PAD_STACK, extra float temporaries, casts, or pragmas in the diff as accepted style; standards require explicit matching evidence and reviewer feedback pushed against pragmas.
Do not claim tools/changes_fmt.py was in the final changed-file set from this slice; it is only present in review feedback examples.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond loaded comments; comments identify likely loops and cleanup requests but not final resolution details.
Do not promote string/data-section movement lessons from this PR beyond the explicit local report; the slice contains data match deltas but not enough ownership reasoning for a data standard.
